free_text: Measurement of activation cross sections of alpha
particle induced reactions on iridium up to an energy
of 50 MeV
Natural Ir (25um), Ti (10.9um) for monitoring
high purity metalic foils
ORTEC GEM35P4-70
Two stacks
consisting of Ir and Ti foils were irradiated with
alpha beam of E=51.27 MeV and E=51.23 MeV energy.
Irradiation were done in a Faraday-cup like
targetholder with beam intensity of I=209nA and I=207nA
for 2.0 h and 1.0 h duration.
The beam intensity was monitored and registered in
every minutes.
No chemical separation was applied.
Several series of spectra were recorded to
be able to follow the decay.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT online data library.
Correction for attenuation of low energy gamma photons
in tha Ir target foils was done.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primari incident beam was
+- 0.2MeV which increased up to +-0.41 MeV in the last
target foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included. Overall uncertainty were about 11-14%.
surface density of target nuclei(1%),
incident charged particle flux (3%),
nuclear decay and branching data(3%)
detector efficiency (3-6%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-7%)
